name: Inventory CSV Import System
scenario: Build a real-world data import system: read a CSV inventory file, validate each row, write accepted rows to a processed report, write rejected rows to an error report. All file I/O uses try-with-resources. Validation uses custom exceptions. The system demonstrates the full Module 7 toolkit in a realistic pipeline.
description: End-to-end CSV import pipeline: try-with-resources for all resources, custom exceptions for validation failures, defensive programming on inputs, structured error reporting. This is what a data import system looks like in production.

core.description: `InventoryRow` record/class: sku (String), name (String), quantity (int), price (double). Static `parse(String csvLine, int rowNum) throws ImportException` — parses the line, throws ImportException for malformed rows (wrong field count, non-numeric qty/price, negative values).

`ImportProcessor.process(String csvData)` — reads all rows using BufferedReader + StringReader in try-with-resources. Parses each row. Accepts valid rows into `List<InventoryRow>`. Rejects invalid rows (catches ImportException per row) into `List<String>` error log.

`ReportWriter.writeReport(List<InventoryRow> rows, List<String> errors)` — writes two reports using PrintWriter + StringWriter in try-with-resources. Accepted report: formatted table with SKU, name, qty, price. Error report: lists each rejected row with reason.
main() calls ImportProcessor then ReportWriter. Prints both reports.
All resources must use try-with-resources (no manual finally for closing).

bonus.description: Add a custom `ReportSession implements AutoCloseable` that tracks import stats (start time, rows processed, rows rejected) and prints a summary on close(). Use it in try-with-resources in main().
Add `totalInventoryValue()` method that returns sum of (quantity × price) for all accepted rows.
Demonstrate suppressed exceptions: create a version of ReportWriter where close() throws, and show the suppressed exception in the output.

data_to_use:
CSV data (7 rows, 3 invalid):
SKU-001,Wireless Mouse,45,799.0
SKU-002,Keyboard,12,1999.0
SKU-003,,8,18999.0          ← invalid: name empty
SKU-004,USB Hub,60,499.0
SKU-005,Monitor,-5,15000.0  ← invalid: negative quantity
SKU-006,Headphones,abc,2999.0 ← invalid: qty not a number
SKU-007,Laptop Stand,25,1299.0
example_output:
=== IMPORT COMPLETE ===
Accepted: 4 rows
Rejected: 3 rows

=== ACCEPTED INVENTORY ===
SKU-001  Wireless Mouse      qty=45  ₹799.0
SKU-002  Keyboard            qty=12  ₹1999.0
SKU-004  USB Hub             qty=60  ₹499.0
SKU-007  Laptop Stand        qty=25  ₹1299.0

=== IMPORT ERRORS ===
Row 3: name cannot be blank
Row 5: quantity must be positive, got: -5
Row 6: quantity must be a number: 'abc'

criteria: Both accepted and error reports (10 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key patterns: InventoryRow.parse() catches NumberFormatException for qty/price and rethrows as ImportException. ImportProcessor wraps the entire read in try-with-resources and per-row in try-catch (so one bad row doesn't stop the batch). Both reports use separate try-with-resources blocks.
submission_instructions: Save as InventoryImport.java. Run it. Push to GitHub — this is a complete real-world data pipeline demonstrating the full Module 7 exception handling toolkit.
tips_for_success: parse() method: split on comma → check length == 4. Then parseInt/parseDouble each field in try-catch → throw ImportException on failure.
ImportProcessor: outer try-with-resources for the BufferedReader. Inner try-catch per row to collect errors without stopping.
ReportWriter: two separate PrintWriter try-with-resources blocks (one for accepted, one for errors). Or two in the same try with semicolons.
Keep the rowNumber counter outside the BufferedReader try (int row = 1; before try, row++ inside loop).
InventoryRow can be a simple class or a Java 16+ record — both work.
concepts_reinforced: Custom exceptions with domain fields (rowNumber, reason)
Per-item exception handling without stopping the batch (T7.1 finally pattern)
